title: cacalia alpina subsp. briquetii
intro: Cacalia alpina subsp. briquetii is a subspecies of the Cacalia alpina plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant is commonly known as Briquet's cacalia or Alpine Cacalia, and it is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Cacalia alpina subsp. briquetii has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal purposes.
